lead.titleZh: 观察研究揭示高级实践物理治疗师模式、早期自我效能与脊髓损伤后勃起功能
lead.titleEn: Observational Studies Reveal Advanced Practice PT Models, Early Self-Efficacy, and Erectile Function Post-SCI
lead.paragraphZh: 今日日报共 9 篇，覆盖 5 个专科方向，其中 5 篇最高 curatedScore 达到 75 分，均为观察研究。骨科与肌骨领域的 2 篇观察研究值得关注，分别从高级实践物理治疗师的模式以及下肢骨折后早期自我效能对结局的影响提供了视角。神经康复方面，一篇关于不完全性创伤性颈脊髓损伤男性患者术后勃起功能恢复预测模型的观察研究为临床提供了新工具。
lead.paragraphEn: Today's 9 entries span 5 specialties, with 5 achieving the top curatedScore of 75, all categorized as observational studies. Two observational studies in orthopedic and musculoskeletal care are noteworthy, offering perspectives on advanced practice physiotherapy models and the impact of early self-efficacy on outcomes following lower extremity fractures. In neurorehabilitation, an observational study on developing a predictive model for postoperative erectile function recovery in male patients with incomplete traumatic cervical spinal cord injury presents a new clinical tool.

items.title: Perspectives on an advanced practice physiotherapy-led model for musculoskeletal care in Canada: A qualitative study of patients and multidisciplinary clinicians.
items.summary: A qualitative study in Canada reveals that while Advanced Practice Physiotherapy (APP) shows promise in musculoskeletal care, systemic barriers limit its integration, highlighting diverse perspectives from patients and clinicians.
items.curatedReason: 加拿大一项定性研究揭示了患者和多学科临床医生对高级实践物理治疗师（APP）主导的肌肉骨骼护理模式的看法。APP在减轻全球医疗系统负担方面表现出潜力，但加拿大在系统整合上仍有障碍。这为希望深入了解 APP 模式在不同国家实施挑战和机遇的同行提供了宝贵视角。
items.titleZh: 加拿大高级实践物理治疗师主导的肌肉骨骼护理模式视角：一项患者和多学科临床医生的定性研究
items.summaryZh: 加拿大一项定性研究显示，患者和多学科临床医生对高级实践物理治疗师（APP）主导的肌肉骨骼护理模式持积极态度，但其在加拿大仍面临系统性整合障碍。
items.curatedReasonEn: This qualitative study from Canada explores the perceptions of patients and multidisciplinary clinicians regarding Advanced Practice Physiotherapy (APP)-led musculoskeletal care. While APP shows promise in reducing healthcare burden globally, Canada faces systemic integration barriers. This offers valuable insight for peers interested in the implementation challenges and opportunities of APP models in various countries.
items.limitation: 本研究为定性研究，结果可能不具备广泛代表性，且受访者数量有限。
items.limitationEn: This is a qualitative study, so its findings may not be broadly generalizable and the number of participants is limited.

items.title: Early Self-Efficacy Predicts Short- and Long-Term Pain and Functional Outcomes Following Lower Extremity Fracture
items.summary: This study aimed to identify which early psychosocial measures, particularly self-efficacy, are most predictive of short- and long-term pain severity, pain interference, physical function, and chronic pain following lower extremity fractures.
items.curatedReason: 这项研究发现早期自我效能感能预测下肢骨折患者短期和长期的疼痛与功能结局。这提示你，在康复早期评估并干预患者的自我效能感，可能对改善其长期康复效果至关重要。
items.titleZh: 早期自我效能感预测下肢骨折后的短期和长期疼痛及功能结局
items.summaryZh: 本研究旨在确定早期心理社会测量，特别是自我效能感，在下肢骨折恢复过程中对疼痛严重程度、疼痛干扰、身体功能和慢性疼痛的短期和长期预测作用。
items.curatedReasonEn: This study found that early self-efficacy predicts short- and long-term pain and functional outcomes in patients with lower extremity fractures. This suggests that assessing and intervening on patients' self-efficacy early in rehabilitation may be crucial for improving long-term rehabilitation outcomes.
items.limitation: 研究未明确说明样本量和随访长度，可能影响结果的强度和普适性。
items.limitationEn: The study does not clearly state the sample size and follow-up duration, which may affect the strength and generalizability of the results.
items.journal: Archives of Physical Medicine and Rehabilitation

items.title: Development of a predictive model for postoperative erectile function recovery in male patients with incomplete traumatic cervical spinal cord injury.
items.summary: A clinical prediction model was developed to forecast postoperative erectile function recovery in male patients with incomplete traumatic cervical spinal cord injury, a prevalent issue lacking sufficient predictive tools.
items.curatedReason: 该研究开发了预测不完全性创伤性颈脊髓损伤男性患者术后勃起功能恢复的模型。这个模型有助于你与患者沟通预后，并为性康复干预提供更早期的风险分层依据。
items.titleZh: 不完全性创伤性颈脊髓损伤男性患者术后勃起功能恢复预测模型的开发
items.summaryZh: 本研究开发了一个临床预测模型，用于预测不完全性创伤性颈脊髓损伤（CSCI）男性患者术后勃起功能恢复，以弥补当前预测工具的不足。
items.curatedReasonEn: This study developed a predictive model for postoperative erectile function recovery in male patients with incomplete traumatic cervical spinal cord injury. This model helps you communicate prognosis to patients and provides earlier risk stratification for sexual rehabilitation interventions.
items.limitation: 研究仅限于男性患者，且需外部验证来确认模型在不同人群中的普适性。
items.limitationEn: The study is limited to male patients, and external validation is needed to confirm the model's generalizability in diverse populations.
items.journal: European spine journal : official publication of the European Spine Society, the European Spinal Deformity Society, and the European Section of the Cervical Spine Research Society

items.title: Early post-hospital trajectories of health-related quality of life for younger adult patients with traumatic brain injury
items.summary: This study characterized the early post-hospital trajectories of health-related quality of life (HRQOL) in younger adult patients with traumatic brain injury (TBI) during the first 8 weeks after discharge from acute care.
items.curatedReason: 这项研究描绘了年轻创伤性脑损伤（TBI）患者出院后早期（8周内）健康相关生活质量的轨迹。这为你提供了 TBI 患者急性期后 HRQOL 恢复模式的关键信息，有助于你更早地识别潜在问题并制定个性化康复计划。
items.titleZh: 年轻成人创伤性脑损伤患者出院后健康相关生活质量的早期轨迹
items.summaryZh: 本研究描述了年轻成人创伤性脑损伤（TBI）患者在急性医院护理出院后8周内，健康相关生活质量（HRQOL）的早期轨迹。
items.curatedReasonEn: This study outlines the early (within 8 weeks) post-discharge trajectories of health-related quality of life for younger adult patients with traumatic brain injury (TBI). This provides you with crucial information on HRQOL recovery patterns after the acute phase in TBI patients, helping you identify potential issues earlier and develop personalized rehabilitation plans.
items.limitation: 研究关注的是早期（8周内）轨迹，缺乏更长期的随访数据来评估远期效果。
items.limitationEn: The study focuses on early trajectories (within 8 weeks) and lacks longer-term follow-up data to assess distant outcomes.
items.journal: Archives of Physical Medicine and Rehabilitation

items.title: Subregion-Specific Prefrontal Activation Predicts Fall Risk and Fall Timing in Older Adults with Mild Cognitive Impairment
items.summary: This study found that subregion-specific prefrontal cortex activation during dual-task walking predicts fall risk and fall timing in older adults with mild cognitive impairment.
items.curatedReason: 这项研究发现，双任务行走时前额叶皮层（PFC）激活模式可预测轻度认知障碍（MCI）老年人的跌倒风险和首次跌倒时间。将神经影像学指标纳入风险评估，有望更精细地识别高风险人群，并指导更精准的预防干预。
items.titleZh: 亚区域特异性前额叶激活可预测轻度认知障碍老年人的跌倒风险和跌倒时间。
items.summaryZh: 这项研究发现，双任务行走时前额叶皮层特定区域的激活可预测轻度认知障碍老年人的跌倒风险和首次跌倒时间。
items.curatedReasonEn: This study found that prefrontal cortex (PFC) activation patterns during dual-task walking can predict fall risk and time to first fall in older adults with mild cognitive impairment (MCI). Integrating neuroimaging markers into risk assessment holds promise for more precise identification of high-risk individuals and guiding targeted preventive interventions.
items.limitation: 研究对象仅限于轻度认知障碍老年人，结果可能不适用于其他人群。
items.limitationEn: The study population is limited to older adults with mild cognitive impairment, so the results may not be generalizable to other populations.
items.journal: Archives of Physical Medicine and Rehabilitation

items.title: Implementing Live Long Walk Strong: Practice-based evidence on adapting a physical therapy program across diverse contexts.
items.summary: A process evaluation describes the adaptation of "Live Long Walk Strong," an age-friendly physical therapy program designed to improve mobility in older adults, across four diverse clinical settings.
items.curatedReason: 这项研究描述了“Live Long Walk Strong”物理治疗项目在不同临床环境中如何进行适应性调整。这为你提供了在老年康复领域跨不同背景实施和调整现有项目的实践证据，有助于你提升项目的可及性和有效性。
items.titleZh: “Live Long Walk Strong”项目的实施：跨多样化背景调整物理治疗项目的实践证据
items.summaryZh: 一项过程评估描述了“Live Long Walk Strong”这一旨在改善老年人活动能力的物理治疗项目，如何在四个不同的临床环境中进行适应性调整，提供了实践证据。
items.curatedReasonEn: This study describes how the "Live Long Walk Strong" physical therapy program was adapted across diverse clinical contexts. This provides you with practice-based evidence for implementing and adapting existing programs in geriatric rehabilitation across different settings, helping you enhance program accessibility and effectiveness.
items.limitation: 本研究为过程评估，主要关注项目适应性，而非干预的最终效果或与对照组的比较。
items.limitationEn: This is a process evaluation, focusing primarily on program adaptability rather than ultimate intervention effects or comparison with control groups.
items.journal: PM & R : the journal of injury, function, and rehabilitation

items.title: Physiotherapy management of Long-COVID: an evidence-based approach.
items.summary: A review provides an evidence-based approach to the physiotherapy management of Long-COVID, a heterogeneous and multisystemic condition following SARS-CoV-2 infection.
items.curatedReason: 这篇综述为Long-COVID的物理治疗管理提供了基于证据的方法。它为你提供了最新的临床框架，帮助你理解并干预这种多系统、异质性强的疾病，但要记住，Long-COVID的证据仍在不断演进。
items.titleZh: Long-COVID的物理治疗管理：一种基于证据的方法
items.summaryZh: 一篇综述为Long-COVID的物理治疗管理提供了一种基于证据的方法，该疾病是一种感染SARS-CoV-2后可能出现的异质性、偶发性、多系统性疾病。
items.curatedReasonEn: This review provides an evidence-based approach to the physiotherapy management of Long-COVID. It offers you an updated clinical framework to understand and intervene in this multisystemic, heterogeneous condition, but remember that Long-COVID evidence is still evolving.
items.limitation: 作为一篇综述，其结论的强度依赖于所引用研究的质量和异质性。
items.limitationEn: As a review, the strength of its conclusions depends on the quality and heterogeneity of the cited studies.

items.title: [Editorial] Migration: a reality, not an emergency
items.summary: An editorial from The Lancet argues that migration is a fundamental human reality, often mislabeled as an emergency, impacting healthcare access for millions.
items.curatedReason: 这篇社论指出了移民问题不仅是全球公共卫生挑战，也影响移民群体的医疗可及性。作为物理治疗师，你需要意识到服务移民患者时可能面临的系统性障碍，如语言、文化差异和对医疗系统的不信任，并倡导建立更具包容性的医疗环境。
items.titleZh: [社论] 移民：现实而非紧急情况
items.summaryZh: 《柳叶刀》社论指出，移民是人类生活最古老的事实之一，却常被视为“紧急情况”，这导致移民在医疗上面临诸多障碍，例如害怕寻求医疗服务、在异国他乡工作强度大等。
items.curatedReasonEn: This editorial highlights that migration is not just a global public health challenge but also affects healthcare accessibility for immigrant populations. As a physical therapist, you need to be aware of systemic barriers when serving immigrant patients, such as language, cultural differences, and distrust of the healthcare system, and advocate for a more inclusive healthcare environment.

items.title: [Perspectives] The unfinished path towards patient safety
items.summary: This 'Perspectives' piece from The Lancet discusses the inherent trust patients place in healthcare systems during surgery and the ongoing path towards achieving robust patient safety.
items.curatedReason: 这篇评论文章强调了患者安全的重要性及其建立在信任之上的本质。作为临床实践者，你需要持续关注并优化患者安全流程，不仅是避免失误，更是维护患者对医疗系统的基本信任，提升患者体验。
items.titleZh: [观点] 患者安全未竟之路
items.summaryZh: 《柳叶刀》的这篇“观点”文章探讨了患者在接受手术时对医疗系统的信任，以及实现完善患者安全所面临的挑战和未竟之路。
items.curatedReasonEn: This commentary emphasizes the critical importance of patient safety, rooted in trust. As a clinician, you must continually focus on and optimize patient safety protocols, not just to prevent errors, but to uphold patients' fundamental trust in the healthcare system and enhance their experience.
